reasoning: Lilly King's 100m breaststroke long course world record (1:04.13) has stood since 2017 and is one of the most resilient records in the sport. As of the knowledge cutoff (October 26, 2025), Wikipedia confirms she still holds the record, having survived the 2024 Olympics and the 2025 World Aquatics Championships (LC) in Singapore (July 2025), where the winning time was in the 1:05-high range (Anna Elendt). The only active swimmer to come close recently is China's Tang Qianting (1:04.39 in 2024), but her 2025 form has been slower. The resolution window (November 2025 - January 2026) falls during the short course (25m) season and the early-season training phase for long course. While the US Open (Dec 2025) will be long course, it is rarely a venue for world records. Given the record survived the peak 2025 competition season and no major long course championships are scheduled in the immediate future, the probability of it holding is very high, with slight decreases as the timeline extends to include secondary meets.
